data.narrative:
Runway is a Series C–stage AI & Software company with a current post-money valuation of $1.5B and $240M+ in total raised capital. The relevant peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), all operating in the same sector and development stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next institutional funding round, implies a valuation of $2.5B. Peer-median methodology across six same-sector, same-stage comparables (Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Neuralink at $9.7B, Luma AI at $4.0B, ElevenLabs at $3.0B, and one additional peer) yields a median of $6.8B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $2.2B (low), $4.7B (median), and $7.9B (high). The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within the peer set—particularly the outsized valuations of Figure AI and Skild AI relative to mid-range comparables. Key uncertainties center on whether Runway's revenue, product adoption, and technical differentiation align with top-tier peers or more conservative comparables, and whether the current market environment for frontier AI justifies peer multiples or implies compression.
